---
title: "Group 1 QIIME2 Workflow"
author: "Simar Jawanda"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  eval = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)
```

# Overview

This file documents the QIIME2 workflow used for Group 1. Group 1 contains the single-end BioProject `PRJNA1209429-preg`, which was later processed into the final Group 1 analysis directory.

# Step 1: Set Project Directory and QIIME2 Container

```bash
PROJ='/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/PRJNA1209429-preg'; cd "$PROJ"
docker pull quay.io/qiime2/amplicon:2026.1
qiime_docker () { docker run --rm -it --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)" -v "$PROJ:/data" -w /data quay.io/qiime2/amplicon:2026.1 qiime "$@"; }
```

# Step 2: Prepare FASTQ Files and Manifest

```bash
cd "$PROJ/fastq"; for f in SRR*.fastq; do [ -e "$f" ] || continue; [ -f "$f.gz" ] || gzip -c "$f" > "$f.gz"; done; cd "$PROJ"
mkdir -p "$PROJ/manifests"
{
  printf "sample-id\tabsolute-filepath\n"
  for f in "$PROJ"/fastq/SRR*.fastq.gz; do
    [ -e "$f" ] || continue
    s=$(basename "$f" .fastq.gz)
    printf "%s\t/data/fastq/%s.fastq.gz\n" "$s" "$s"
  done | sort -V
} > "$PROJ/manifests/se.tsv"
head -n 5 "$PROJ/manifests/se.tsv" | cat -vet
```

# Step 3: Import Reads and Summarize Demultiplexed Data

```bash
qiime_docker tools import \
  --type 'SampleData[SequencesWithQuality]' \
  --input-path manifests/se.tsv \
  --input-format SingleEndFastqManifestPhred33V2 \
  --output-path reads.qza

qiime_docker demux summarize \
  --i-data reads.qza \
  --o-visualization reads.qzv
```

# Step 4: Test Primer Matching and Trim Reads

The workbook notes show that several forward primers were tested. The best-supported match was the 338F primer because it gave a 100% match with about 91.3% to 91.4% bases retained.

```bash
qiime_docker cutadapt trim-single \
  --i-demultiplexed-sequences reads.qza \
  --p-front ACTCCTACGGGAGGCAGCAG \
  --verbose \
  --o-trimmed-sequences trim_test_338.qza
```

Notes from primer testing:

- 338F was the strongest match and was treated as the final primer choice.
- 341F was also detected at high frequency, but 338F performed slightly better.
- 515F overtrimmed the reads.
- 27F and 799F were not detected.

Final trimming:

```bash
qiime_docker cutadapt trim-single \
  --i-demultiplexed-sequences reads.qza \
  --p-front ACTCCTACGGGAGGCAGCAG \
  --p-discard-untrimmed \
  --verbose \
  --o-trimmed-sequences trim.qza

qiime_docker demux summarize \
  --i-data trim.qza \
  --o-visualization trim.qzv
```

Trim note: only about 0.02% of reads were discarded, so 99.98% of reads were retained after final trimming.

# Step 5: Run DADA2 Denoising

```bash
cd "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429"
export PROJ="/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA"

qiime_docker dada2 denoise-single \
  --i-demultiplexed-seqs "/data/PRJNA1209429-preg/trim.qza" \
  --p-trim-left 0 \
  --p-trunc-len 260 \
  --p-max-ee 2 \
  --p-n-threads 0 \
  --o-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-dada2.qza" \
  --o-representative-sequences "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-dada2.qza" \
  --o-denoising-stats "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/stats-dada2.qza" \
  --o-base-transition-stats "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/base-transition-stats.qza"
```

DADA2 note:

- `260` was chosen as the truncation length because 270 was the last clearly high-quality position and a 10 bp safety buffer was used.
- This was single-end data, so overlap did not need to be considered.
- `--p-n-threads 0` was used to allow DADA2 to use all available cores.

```bash
qiime_docker metadata tabulate \
  --m-input-file "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/stats-dada2.qza" \
  --o-visualization "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/stats-dada2.qzv"
```

Retention note:

- Passed-filter values were about 82% to 85%.
- Final non-chimeric counts were roughly 39k to 47k reads per sample.

# Step 6: Convert Metadata for QIIME2

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import csv
from pathlib import Path

inp = Path("/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/metadata.csv")
out = Path("/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/metadata_qiime.tsv")

with inp.open(encoding="utf-8-sig", newline="") as f:
    r = csv.DictReader(f)
    cols = r.fieldnames or []
    other = [c for c in cols if c != "Run"]
    with out.open("w", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as g:
        w = csv.writer(g, delimiter="\t")
        w.writerow(["#SampleID"] + other)
        for row in r:
            sid = (row.get("Run") or "").strip()
            if sid:
                w.writerow([sid] + [row.get(c, "") for c in other])

print("Wrote:", out)
PY
```

# Step 7: Summarize the DADA2 Table and Representative Sequences

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table summarize \
  --i-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-dada2.qza" \
  --m-metadata-file "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/metadata_qiime.tsv" \
  --o-feature-frequencies "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/feature-frequencies-dada2.qza" \
  --o-sample-frequencies "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/sample-frequencies-dada2.qza" \
  --o-summary "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-dada2.qzv"

qiime_docker feature-table tabulate-seqs \
  --i-data "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-dada2.qza" \
  --o-visualization "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-dada2.qzv"
```

# Step 8: Filter Low-Abundance Features

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table filter-features \
  --i-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-dada2.qza" \
  --p-min-frequency 43 \
  --p-min-samples 2 \
  --o-filtered-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-filt.qza"

qiime_docker feature-table filter-seqs \
  --i-data "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-dada2.qza" \
  --i-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-filt.qza" \
  --o-filtered-data "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-filt.qza"
```

Filtering note: the workbook listed a calculated threshold of `43.3334`, which was implemented as `43`.

# Step 9: Copy SILVA Reference Files and Assign Taxonomy

```bash
cp "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/PRJNA831840/silva-138-99-seqs.qza" \
   "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/"

cp "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/PRJNA831840/silva-138-99-tax.qza" \
   "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/"

qiime_docker feature-classifier classify-consensus-vsearch \
  --i-query "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-filt.qza" \
  --i-reference-reads "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/silva-138-99-seqs.qza" \
  --i-reference-taxonomy "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/silva-138-99-tax.qza" \
  --p-perc-identity 0.97 \
  --p-query-cov 0.8 \
  --p-threads 8 \
  --o-classification "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy.qza" \
  --o-search-results "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy_hits.qza"

qiime_docker metadata tabulate \
  --m-input-file "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy.qza" \
  --o-visualization "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy.qzv"
```

# Step 10: Remove Mitochondria and Chloroplast Features

```bash
qiime_docker taxa filter-table \
  --i-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-filt.qza" \
  --i-taxonomy "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy.qza" \
  --p-include p__ \
  --p-exclude mitochondria,chloroplast \
  --o-filtered-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-no-mc.qza"

qiime_docker taxa filter-seqs \
  --i-sequences "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-filt.qza" \
  --i-taxonomy "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy.qza" \
  --p-include p__ \
  --p-exclude mitochondria,chloroplast \
  --o-filtered-sequences "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-no-mc.qza"

qiime_docker feature-table tabulate-seqs \
  --i-data "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-no-mc.qza" \
  --o-visualization "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-no-mc.qzv"
```

Verification note: the first five sequences in the filtered representative-sequence file were checked by BLAST and matched microbial sequences.

```bash
qiime_docker feature-table summarize \
  --i-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-no-mc.qza" \
  --m-metadata-file "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/metadata_qiime.tsv" \
  --o-feature-frequencies "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-no-mc-feature-freq.qza" \
  --o-sample-frequencies "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-no-mc-sample-freq.qza" \
  --o-summary "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-no-mc.qzv"
```

# Step 11: Collapse to Genus Level and Build Relative-Abundance Outputs

```bash
qiime_docker taxa collapse \
  --i-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-no-mc.qza" \
  --i-taxonomy "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy.qza" \
  --p-level 6 \
  --o-collapsed-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-genus.qza"

qiime_docker feature-table relative-frequency \
  --i-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-genus.qza" \
  --o-relative-frequency-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-genus-relfreq.qza"

qiime_docker taxa barplot \
  --i-table "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-no-mc.qza" \
  --i-taxonomy "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy.qza" \
  --m-metadata-file "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/metadata_qiime.tsv" \
  --o-visualization "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxa-barplot.qzv"
```

# Step 12: Export Tables, Sequences and Denoising Outputs

```bash
conda create -n biom_env -c conda-forge -c bioconda python=3.10 biom-format -y
conda activate biom_env

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-genus-relfreq.qza" \
  --output-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-genus-relfreq"

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/taxonomy.qza" \
  --output-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-taxonomy"

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/table-no-mc.qza" \
  --output-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-asv-table"

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/rep-seqs-no-mc.qza" \
  --output-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-rep-seqs"

biom convert \
  -i "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-genus-relfreq/feature-table.biom" \
  -o "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-genus-relfreq/genus-relfreq.tsv" \
  --to-tsv

biom convert \
  -i "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-asv-table/feature-table.biom" \
  -o "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-asv-table/asv-table.tsv" \
  --to-tsv

awk 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="\t"} NR==1{print "#OTUID","taxonomy"; next} {print $1,$2}' \
"/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-taxonomy/taxonomy.tsv" \
> "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-taxonomy/taxonomy_for_biom.tsv"

biom add-metadata \
  -i "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-asv-table/feature-table.biom" \
  -o "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.biom" \
  --observation-metadata-fp "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-taxonomy/taxonomy_for_biom.tsv" \
  --sc-separated taxonomy

biom convert \
  -i "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.biom" \
  -o "/Users/simarjawanda/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/QIIME2 DATA/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-asv-table/feature-table_w_tax.txt" \
  --to-tsv \
  --header-key taxonomy

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "/data/PRJNA1209429-preg/trim.qza" \
  --output-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-trimmed-reads"

qiime_docker tools export \
  --input-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/stats-dada2.qza" \
  --output-path "/data/Group_1_PRJNA1209429/exported-denoising-stats"
```
